goal: Highest-standard Lean formal verification for Hegemon as a PQC privacy chain, with critical theorem targets proved and production-bound at little to no performance cost.
completion_method: Weighted average of property completion percentages. A property reaches 100 only when the top-level Lean theorem is proved, assumptions are explicit, production conformance/refinement gates bind the shipped path, formal-core includes the gate, and performance-sensitive runtime changes are benchmarked or demonstrably cheap.

last_coordinator_audit.summary: Audits confirmed strong Lean hygiene, admission/vector framework, nullifier and balance guardrails, parser/canonical encoding evidence, replay/admission/network posture, and release gates. They also confirmed the highest-value gaps remain proof-system soundness, spend authorization, privacy/confidentiality games, bridge production mint safety, cryptographic primitive assumptions, and full Rust/native-node refinement.
critical_open_gaps: No end-to-end theorem that accepted spends imply authorized private witnesses.
No in-repo proof-system soundness theorem for the deployed proving stack or full AIR-to-spec equivalence.
No formal privacy, zero-knowledge, unlinkability, or note-ciphertext confidentiality game.
No complete Rust/native-node refinement from raw network/RPC bytes through storage, reorg, restart, and accepted state publication.
Bridge local replay binding is stronger than production mint safety; external proof soundness and release verifier posture remain limiting.

last_theorem_slice.summary: Added `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer` as an explicit shielded-transaction observer/leakage model. The theorems prove that changing private witnesses or prover randomness does not change the observer view, and that equal public inputs, ciphertext wire observations, and placement imply equal allowed leakage. The slice is proof-only and non-production-eligible; simulator-based ZK, ciphertext confidentiality, wallet metadata, and timing/network privacy remain open assumptions/targets.
known_limitations: The observer model is an extensional leakage boundary, not a simulator-based zero-knowledge proof.
Does not prove ML-KEM/AEAD confidentiality, ciphertext indistinguishability, wallet metadata hygiene, timing privacy, network privacy, or complete Rust refinement.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Every accepted native chain preserves the native supply equation: genesis supply plus authorized subsidies and accepted coinbase fees minus explicit burns equals the claimed supply digest.
missing_work: Bind all native import, mined-block, announced-block, sync, reorg, startup-repair, and replay paths to the theorem-backed accepted-chain transition.
State cryptographic and proof-system assumptions explicitly as theorem parameters.
properties.performance_constraint: No extra heavy runtime work; reuse existing checked supply helpers and replay summaries.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Every accepted chain has globally unique spent nullifiers and rejects within-block, cross-block, pending, replay, and reorg duplicate nullifiers.
current_evidence: pending-action reload and transfer-state admission gates
formal/lean/Hegemon/Native/AcceptedChain.lean accepted native replay-chain nullifiers Nodup theorem
formal/lean/Hegemon/Native/ActionStreamEffect.lean accepted stream imports preserve imported-nullifier Nodup theorem
missing_work: Bind canonical replay, startup reload, side-branch canonicalization, and pending quarantine to the same carried nullifier transition relation.
Add hostile reorg/startup duplicate-nullifier regressions if any import path lacks them.
properties.performance_constraint: Use existing nullifier set membership checks; avoid extra scans beyond replay planning already required.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Every accepted spend has an authorized private witness: valid note secret/opening, spend authority, nullifier derivation, Merkle membership, and proof acceptance for the exact public statement.
missing_work: Mechanize the transaction relation that connects witnesses to public inputs.
Prove that the circuit/AIR constraints imply the spend-authorization relation.
Tie proof-system soundness assumptions to accepted spend authorization.
properties.performance_constraint: Proof-side theorem only; production should continue verifying the same proof/artifact bytes.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Accepted state roots equal ordered insertion of accepted note commitments, and every accepted Merkle membership proof opens to a prior committed note.
missing_work: Unify Merkle membership, tree transition, native replay planning, and startup reload into one chain-level tree invariant.
Prove reorg replay and startup repair preserve the same canonical commitment tree as genesis replay.
properties.performance_constraint: Do not add duplicate tree recomputation on hot import except in existing replay/repair paths.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: For every accepted non-mint transaction, each asset's input value equals output value plus fees and burns; authorized mint exceptions are explicit and separately checked.
current_evidence: public input shape and binding gates
missing_work: Replace the current AcceptedProofArtifact balance-soundness assumption with a proved AIR/proof-system soundness theorem.
Separate native asset, stablecoin, and bridge asset conservation rules.
properties.performance_constraint: Formal proof only or cheap public-input checks; do not add per-proof duplicate arithmetic beyond existing verifier inputs.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: No accepted transaction can transform one asset into another; stablecoin and bridge mint/burn deltas require explicit authorized evidence.
current_evidence: stablecoin fields in transaction public-input binding
missing_work: State asset isolation as a chain-level theorem.
Formalize stablecoin issuance and bridge mint authorization exceptions as separate predicates.
Bind exception predicates to production admission paths.
properties.performance_constraint: Use already-public asset and policy fields; avoid scanning private data.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Every accepted proof artifact verifies exactly the consensus statement used for nullifiers, commitments, fees, asset deltas, public-input digest, backend profile, and block commitment.
current_evidence: transaction, consensus, SuperNeo, aggregation V5, and block commitment shared-helper gates
missing_work: Lift the accepted-proof-artifact theorem beyond balance facts to every consensus statement field.
Complete bindings for any remaining production proof artifact entry point.
State hash collision resistance and transcript assumptions explicitly.
properties.performance_constraint: Keep shared helper reuse; no extra hashing beyond already-required statement construction.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: If the shipped proof verifier accepts a transaction, tx-leaf, receipt-root, or recursive-block artifact, then the corresponding abstract relation holds, subject to explicit STARK/hash/lattice assumptions.
current_evidence: native backend review-policy and release-posture gates
native tx-leaf and receipt-root artifact parser/schedule gates
AcceptedProofArtifact names the proof-system balance-soundness assumption used to lift balance facts
missing_work: Mechanize the transaction AIR relation or an auditable abstraction of it.
Mechanize or explicitly parameterize STARK/FRI soundness assumptions.
Mechanize native lattice backend binding/fold assumptions or keep the backend out of production claims.
Prove artifact acceptance implies the abstract ledger relation.
properties.performance_constraint: Mostly proof work; any runtime admission additions must be cheap prechecks.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Public chain data and accepted proofs reveal only declared public outputs, and same-wallet/same-recipient worlds are indistinguishable except through public timing and explicitly public metadata.
current_evidence: privacy-oriented design in DESIGN.md and METHODS.md
public/private field separation in transaction statement docs
missing_work: State ZK/privacy assumptions for the proof system and note encryption.
Add unlinkability tests for wallet metadata, ciphertext format, proof wrappers, and sidecars.
Prove or parameterize simulator-based zero-knowledge.
properties.performance_constraint: Avoid adding padding or dummy traffic unless explicitly benchmarked and accepted.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Accepted note ciphertexts decrypt only for intended recipients, bind to the committed note contents, and malformed ciphertexts fail closed.
current_evidence: formal/lean/Hegemon/Privacy/Observer.lean includes ciphertext bytes and parsed summaries in observer leakage
wallet note ciphertext wire vector gate
DA sidecar ciphertext hash and staged-ciphertext reload gates
missing_work: Formalize note plaintext-to-commitment binding at the wallet/chain boundary.
Parameterize ML-KEM and symmetric encryption confidentiality assumptions.
Add recipient/wrong-key/malleated-ciphertext regression coverage if absent.
properties.performance_constraint: No extra chain verification of private plaintext; keep correctness checks in wallet tests and public hash bindings.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Consensus and proof-admission byte objects have exact canonical encodings; trailing bytes, alternate encodings, nested wrappers, and parser differentials reject.
missing_work: Complete byte-level grammar coverage for every consensus object.
Move remaining bincode/trust-boundary encodings to bounded canonical codecs or prove exact wrappers.
Add a top-level parser non-malleability theorem family.
properties.performance_constraint: Canonical decode checks should remain linear and bounded before allocation-heavy work.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Live import, announced import, sync import, reorg, restart, and repair states equal replaying the accepted canonical chain from genesis.
current_evidence: atomic commit manifest and storage durability gates
missing_work: Extend the accepted-chain lift into one Lean native ledger transition relation covering commitments, sidecars, bridge replay, and storage reload.
Bind every production import and startup publication path to that transition.
Add reorg/restart adversarial cases for supply, nullifiers, commitments, sidecars, and bridge replay together.
properties.performance_constraint: Refinement proof should reuse existing replay planning; avoid extra full replay on normal block import.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: No block enters the accepted chain unless version, PoW, timestamp, retarget, miner identity, proof policy, supply, action root, DA root, and artifact checks pass in required order.
current_evidence: PoW, header, version-policy, miner-identity, proof-policy, mined-work, announced-block, block-commitment, and work-template gates
implementation order and dominance constraints in blueprint
missing_work: Unify admission components into one accepted-block theorem.
Make order/dominance constraints theorem-indexed rather than only blueprint-indexed.
Add explicit fork-choice safety theorem boundary.
properties.performance_constraint: Order checks are compile/CI evidence; runtime order should stay as current cheap prechecks before heavy work.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Accepted blocks bind exactly the sidecar ciphertext/proof bytes required for private-state recovery and reject missing, swapped, oversized, or hash-mismatched sidecars before mutation.
missing_work: Lift sidecar gates into a chain-level recoverability theorem.
Bind DA-root construction and sidecar materialization across mining, announced import, sync, and replay.
Specify hot/cold DA retention assumptions outside consensus.
properties.performance_constraint: Keep sidecar checks bounded by existing size caps and staged hashes.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Inbound bridge minting is accepted at most once, only for a valid external proof/checkpoint, and only for the authorized message amount and destination.
current_evidence: RISC Zero release verifier fail-closed gate
missing_work: Define bridge mint authorization theorem independent of the current disabled RISC Zero verifier.
Mechanize or parameterize external-chain proof soundness.
Prove replay-key uniqueness and supply/mint coupling for bridge assets.
properties.performance_constraint: Keep release verifier fail-closed until a PQ-clean verifier exists; avoid classical verifier runtime costs.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: P2P secure channels derive secret, direction-separated keys from authenticated PQ handshakes, never reuse nonces under a key, and reject downgrade/legacy modes.
current_evidence: prior ML-KEM seed and AES-GCM direction separation fixes
missing_work: Add a full channel-state theorem for nonce monotonicity and direction separation.
Bind all active frame send/receive paths to the theorem-backed key schedule.
State ML-KEM/ML-DSA/HKDF/AES-GCM assumptions explicitly.
properties.performance_constraint: No extra round trips; checks should remain key-schedule/frame-admission local.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Every consensus-facing parser, RPC admission path, proof artifact, and sidecar enforces bounded size and bounded pre-verification work before allocation-heavy or proof-heavy work.
missing_work: Unify resource bounds into a global pre-heavy-work theorem.
Add explicit cost classes for every public input family.
Benchmark any new runtime check on hegemon-dev or local release builds.
properties.performance_constraint: The property itself enforces little-to-no performance cost by putting cheap caps before expensive work.

properties.top_level_theorem_target: Release artifacts are accepted only when source, dependency, binary, and native-backend posture policies are satisfied, with explicit waivers and no hidden classical/PQ-forbidden paths.
current_evidence: native backend review-policy and release-posture gates
formal-core dependency and release posture checks
missing_work: Move remaining advisory/waiver drift into mandatory CI if not already enforced for all release workflows.
Add binary scanner completeness review and accepted external-review artifact requirements.
Tie release branch protection to formal-core results.
properties.performance_constraint: Release-time only; no production runtime overhead.
